props.covers: Transient dogfood log for the 2026-07-10 docs-content session: every friction point found while authoring, tagged with its lane (1 = docs content, 2 = docs-system, 3 = canvas code) and its resolution. Deliverable back to the docs-system project; retired when the session lands.

text.insert: Findings Log (docs-content session, 2026-07-10)

One entry per friction point, in discovery order. Lanes: 1 = canvas docs content (fixed here), 2 = docs-system (fix in the docs-system session/batch pass), 3 = canvas code (queued only — the tree is frozen).
F0 — Submodule setup needs a one-level nested init (lane 2, resolved)

Pre-logged from the layout session: the original setup instructions lacked the one-level `git submodule update --init packages/canvas` inside tools/docs-framework, and `bun install` fails without it (and recursive init is forbidden — the embedding is circular). DOCS-OVERHAUL.md now records the correct sequence.
F1 — links check rejects directory references (lane 2, queued)

`bun run docs links check` reports ~22 source references as "Source file not found" even though the targets exist on disk — they are directories (packages/canvas/src/connectors/pathfinding, packages/canvas/src/ui, canvases, board-design-reference, several __tests__ dirs). The checker only accepts files, so legitimate directory citations can never go green. Needs either directory support in the checker or a documented rule that references must point at files.
F2 — Two migration-era doc links used a title as a path (lane 1, fixed)

10-system-design/00-overview and 20-implementation/60-chrome both linked to "20-implementation/70-studio-and-canvas JSON files" — a space-containing doc TITLE fragment where a bundle PATH belongs, presumably minted by the .mdx migration. Fixed in this session's structure pass; worth a migrate-time validation that doc-link paths resolve.
F3 — Running server does not pick up new route code (lane 2, queued)

A workbench started the same morning 404s on GET /api/blocks — the agent edit-surface discovery route — because the submodule checkout advanced after boot and `docs serve` runs route code as-of-boot. An agent probing a live server cannot tell "route missing" from "server stale". Wants either a version/route manifest on /api, or serve --dev watching server source, or restart guidance in the workbench.
F4 — No way to create a new doc bundle through the system (lane 2, queued)

Neither the CLI nor the API exposes a create-bundle operation (POST /api/ops mutates existing bundles; migrate only converts .mdx twins). The 05-theme and 90-findings-log shells in this session had to be hand-authored doc.json files. Wants a `docs new <path> --title` CLI command or a POST create route, seeding the root/overview block shape.
F5 — Markdown render emits an empty code fence for the root overview block (lane 2, queued)

GET /api/markdown renders the root overview block (covers/concepts props) as an empty ``` fence right after the H1 — seen on 00-foundation/00-overview. The overview metadata should either render as a proper summary block or be skipped in markdown output.
F6 — Markdown render flattens inline source references to raw paths (lane 2, queued)

In /api/markdown output, a rich-text run like [schema.ts -> reference to document.ts] renders as the raw path inline ('schema.ts defines packages/canvas/src/state/schema/document.ts, the persisted shape…'), and embedded canvases render as '<!-- canvas: (missing src) -->'. Fine for grep, but it makes rendered markdown awkward to read and audit; references deserve a label-preserving markdown form.
F7 — Source comments still cite the deleted OBJECT-DEF-OVERHAUL.md (lane 3, queued)

The decision record OBJECT-DEF-OVERHAUL.md was retired in the layout session, but code comments still cite it as live: state/schema/colors.ts, theme/palette.ts, theme/tokens.ts, state/schema/object-types.ts, objects/geometry.ts, objects/object-defaults.ts, objects/object-def.ts, state/actions/reducer.ts (D17), among others. Queued for a code pass: repoint those comments at the docs/ sections that now own the rationale. No code changes this session — the tree is frozen.
F8 — D2 and D9 rationale not cited anywhere in 10-shape-api (lane 1, in progress)

10-shape-api claims the span D1–D19 and restates/cites 17 of the 19 decision ids inline, but D2 and D9 never appear. The cross-cutting D1–D19 audit will verify their rationale survives somewhere in docs/ and recover it from OBJECT-DEF-OVERHAUL.md's git history if not.
F9 — ConnectionLabelChip is dead code (lane 3, queued)

While widening the connectors doc, Codex verified that packages/canvas/src/connectors/ConnectionLabelChip.tsx is exported but never imported or mounted anywhere under packages/canvas/src — connection labels render exclusively through Connector.tsx's inline SVG label group. A host-supplied worldOverlay could mount the chip and double-render labels. Queued for a code pass: delete the component or wire it as the intended world-overlay label. The tree is frozen this session; the connectors doc documents the inline label as the one live renderer.
F10 — The read-only viewer mounts pointer-capturing resize handles (lane 3, queued)

stage/viewer/InteractiveCanvasViewer.tsx passes only document/viewport/selectedObjectIds into InteractionFeedbackScreen, so every interaction-gated overlay collapses — except SelectionBox, which renders with interactiveHandles=true. The read-only surface therefore draws resize handles with pointer-events:auto that are wired to nothing. Related open design question from the layout session's boundary gaps: should the viewer show a selection box at all (stage/viewer importing the editor's InteractionFeedback is the recorded TODO(layout) exception)? Queued alongside that decision.
F11 — v2-flow fixture drifted from its parity test (lane 3, queued)

The 70-studio audit confirmed v2-flow.canvas.json was edited after v2-flow-figjam-canvas.test.ts's assertions were written: the test expects 40 objects (file has 39), a 10-icon roster (file has 5), an 8px emphasis strokeWidth and explicit waypoints that are gone. These are the '3x v2-flow JSON' failures in the frozen red baseline. Queued for a code session: update the assertions to the current scene (prune, don't regenerate). The studio doc now tells the honest version of the drift story instead of claiming the test catches drift.
